title: Choose chunking to match retrieval granularity
description: Size and overlap chunks so each embedded unit is self-contained enough to match queries without diluting the vector.
body: Use when splitting documents before embedding. Do not embed whole documents or tiny fragments blindly — oversized chunks average away the relevant signal, while fragments lose the context that makes them matchable. Actions: chunk on semantic boundaries with modest overlap, keep each chunk focused on one idea, and benchmark recall across chunk sizes on a labeled set. Validation signals include a recall comparison across chunk strategies and chunks whose retrieved text actually answers the query. Risk boundary: poor chunking caps achievable recall no matter the index; if recall is low and chunks are huge or fragmented, treat chunking as the root cause before tuning the index.
